title: NOC 62023 – Customer and Information Services Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: In Canada’s service-oriented economy, NOC 62023 (Customer and Information Services Supervisors) are the bridge between corporate excellence and client satisfaction. These TEER 2 professionals manage teams in call centers, information hubs, and corporate service departments. At GTR Immigration, we recognize this code as a vital entry point for skilled communicators and leaders who want to secure Permanent Residency while working in a high-growth corporate environment.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6202 – Service supervisorsNOC Code62023

duties: Supervise and coordinate the activities of customer service representatives and information clerks.
Resolve complex customer inquiries and complaints escalated by staff.
Monitor performance metrics and implement strategies to improve service quality.
Recruit and train staff in company procedures and communication techniques.
Prepare reports on customer interactions and service trends.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A university degree or college diploma in business or a related field is often preferred.
Several years of experience in customer service or call centre operations are mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Good. As Canadian businesses expand their international reach, the need for high-level customer service supervision remains a top priority.
labour_market_demand: The demand for information supervisors is driven by the digital transformation of banking, insurance, and telecommunications. Tech hubs like Toronto and Kitchener-Waterloo are consistently hiring for these roles to support global customer bases.
Corporate Growth: Access to leadership positions in Fortune 500 companies.
Tech Integration: Work with advanced CRM systems and AI-driven customer support tools.
Work-Life Balance: Many roles offer hybrid or remote working options in Canada.
pathways: Express Entry (CEC): The most common path for those with Canadian work experience.
Saskatchewan INP: Frequently targets supervisors in demand.
OINP Human Capital Priorities: For high-scoring candidates in service management.
